mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real GDP growth and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, depressing equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative SPX forward returns in more than 55% of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, a sustained drop in industrial production indicates supply chain bottlenecks rather than weak demand, leading to stagflationary equity repricing over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS remains above 146000 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when INDPRO falls below 104.0 while PAYEMS exceeds 146000 over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect increased consumer confidence and construction activity, which historically precede positive corporate earnings revisions and lift equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST changes and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no significant positive drift.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 245.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL crosses above 245.0 compared to periods below that threshold (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in payroll employment and contraction in consumer credit outstanding indicates a structural shift toward savings-driven consumption, which historically supports defensive equity sectors and lifts broad market returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and RSAFS contraction fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in 5-day SPX forward returns over any 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, a sustained increase in industrial production indicates resilient capacity utilization that offsets financing costs, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive relationship with INDPRO when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent over a 3-year rolling window.
